newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6, 2026. The record SEK 2.1bn US AI data-center cooling order (CDUs + over-the-rack air handlers for an AI factory build-out, booked into Q2 2026 order intake) was announced in late April, and the small Optifarm/Speria acquisition closed June 3 - both predate the window. Today's -4.99% move has no identifiable fundamental driver and looks flow-driven (Nordic industrial/AI-cooling sentiment), leaving YTD at just +3.5% despite a record DCT order book.

sources.label: PRNewswire - Munters wins record data-center orders totaling 2.1 BSEK (late April 2026)
Munters press releases (checked for items since June 6; latest is June 3 Optifarm acquisition)

verdicts.rankRationale: A near-5% no-news sell-off that resets YTD to +3.5% improves asymmetry on a latent-node alpha list while the record DCT order book (SEK 2.1bn April win, deliveries 2027-28) keeps the thermal bottleneck thesis intact. Yesterday's down move was a mechanical score reconciliation; today's flow-driven dump changes the entry setup, warranting a modest reversal.
verdicts.bull: Record data-center cooling backlog with CDU/liquid-cooling content scaling into 2027-28 deliveries, yet the stock trades barely above flat YTD - the AI rerating has been given back without any thesis damage.
verdicts.neutral: Data-center exposure is already visible to the market and DCT margins slipped in Q1; the stock is a known AI-cooling name, so residual hidden alpha is moderate rather than deep.
verdicts.bear: Order lumpiness and competitive intensity in CDUs/CRAHs (Vertiv, Boyd, nVent) could compress margins, and a colocation capex pause would hit the 2027 delivery schedule.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 order intake (booking the SEK 2.1bn AI-factory order) and further hyperscale/colo cooling wins.
verdicts.primaryRisk: DCT margin dilution on customized orders plus a data-center capex digestion phase stalling new bookings.

verdicts.rankRationale: Already rank 1 on the power/cooling bottleneck list, so no upward move is possible; a -5% flow-driven day with the cooling bottleneck thesis fully intact slightly improves the risk/reward and supports the top slot rather than challenging it.
verdicts.bull: Direct exposure to the scarcest AI infrastructure inputs - liquid cooling, CDUs, air treatment - with a record order book and a YTD reset to +3.5% that restores entry asymmetry at the top of the list.
verdicts.neutral: No new evidence since June 6; the April order is already in consensus and the rank-1 position already encodes the bottleneck premium.
verdicts.bear: If today's weakness is the start of a broader AI-cooling derating or reflects unannounced order-pipeline softness, the rank-1 score is exposed to a multi-week drawdown.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 report confirming record order intake and DCT margin trajectory.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Margin slippage on customized data-center orders and cyclicality in non-DCT segments masking the bottleneck story.
